2603.25187.title: Probing the Lack of Stable Internal Beliefs in LLMs
2603.25187.paper_summary: Persona-driven large language models (LLMs) require consistent behavioral tendencies across interactions to simulate human-like personality traits, such as persistence or reliability. However, current LLMs often lack stable internal representations that anchor their responses over extended dialogues. This work explores whether LLMs can maintain "implicit consistency", defined as persistent adherence to an unstated goal in multi-turn interactions. We designed a 20-question-style riddle game paradigm where an LLM is tasked with secretly selecting a target and responding to users' guesses with "yes/no" answers. Through evaluations, we find that LLMs struggle to preserve latent consistency: their implicit "goals" shift across turns unless explicitly provided their selected target in context. These findings highlight critical limitations in the building of persona-driven LLMs and underscore the need for mechanisms that anchor implicit goals over time, which is a key to realistic personality modeling in interactive applications such as dialogue systems.
2603.25187.paper_summary_zh: 個人驅動的大型語言模型（LLMs）需要在互動中保持一致的行為傾向，以模擬人類的個性特徵，例如堅持性或可靠性。<lb/>然而，目前的LLMs往往缺乏穩定的內部表徵，無法在長時間的對話中固定其回應。<lb/>本研究探討LLMs是否能夠維持「隱性一致性」，定義為在多輪互動中持續遵循未明言的目標。<lb/>我們設計了一個20題風格的謎語遊戲範式，讓LLM秘密選擇一個目標並以「是/否」的答案回應用戶的猜測。<lb/>通過評估，我們發現LLMs在保持潛在一致性方面存在困難：除非明確提供其選擇的目標，否則它們的隱性「目標」會在回合之間轉變。<lb/>這些發現突顯了在構建個人驅動的LLMs方面的關鍵限制，並強調了需要機制來隨時間固定隱性目標，這是實現互動應用（如對話系統）中現實個性建模的關鍵。
2603.25187.author: Yifan Luo et.al.
2603.25187.authors: Yifan Luo, Kangping Xu, Yanzhen Lu, Yang Yuan, Andrew Chi-Chih Yao
